Uniface HTTP Processing

Uniface HTTP Processing

HTTP is an internet protocol that provides a way for a client to send a request to a server and for the server to return a response. It is used in web applications, mobile apps, and web services to transfer data in a defined request-response cycle. This topic describes how HTTP is used in Uniface applications.

In Uniface applications:

  • The HTTP client, also known as a user agent, can be a web browser, Uniface mobile app, or the UHTTP component.

    Web browsers and mobile apps have a user interface that enables the user to directly interact with the application. The UHTTP component provides operations that can be used in any Uniface application to implement basic browser functionality or retrieve and store data in the cloud.

  • The HTTP server is the Uniface Web Application Server, which provides access to dynamic and static server page components.
  • The dynamic and static server page components execute operations on behalf of the client, and generate a response that usually contains an HTML page to be displayed in the browser.

HTTP Requests

In its simplest form, an HTTP request is a URL to which a message is sent. The URL identifies a particular component or other resource. For more information, see Uniface URL Format.

The message consists of a number of headers that describe the body of the message, followed by the message body itself. The message body may be a query string, or a block of data, such as occurrences that a user has modified in the browser and now wants to store.

Uniface transparently converts HTTP requests into data structures that can be interpreted by the server page components, and converts the generated response back into an HTTP response, ProcScript commands and functions in server page components are used to read and process the requests and generate the responses. For more information, see ProcScript for Dynamic Server Pages and ProcScript for Static Server Pages.

HTTP Reponses

An HTTP response has a similar structure, with headers describing the message followed by message itself. The response of a static server page is almost always a complete HTML page. (Static server pages can also be used to enable file downloads, so the message could conceivably be a file.) The response of a dynamic server page is a complete HTML page the first time the component is requested, but thereafter requests it may contain only the part of the page that has changed.

When the client requests a DSP for the first time, the generated response includes a JavaScript runtime engine that manages and synchronizes subsequent communication between the client and server. It can submit requests to activate server-side Proc modules, and execute responses from the server to execute for client-side JavaScript. For more information, see DSP Component Instances.

HTTP Communication Between Client and Server

The following illustration shows the communication flow in the request-response cycle of a Uniface web or mobile application.

Related Links
  • UHTTP

    The Uniface UHTTP component is an HTTP client that is used for sending and receiving HTTP requests and responses. It provides operations for executing HTTP requests used for web applications and RESTful web services.

  • $webinfo

    Return or set information about the current HTTP request or response.

  • Uniface Web Applications

    Uniface web applications run in a Uniface Web Application Server and are invoked by the client browser over the internet.

  • USYSHTTP

    The USYSHTTP component is a global web application request processor that handles all web requests sent to a Uniface Server via the Uniface Web Request Dispatcher (WRD).

The message consists of a number of headers that describe the body of the message, followed by the message body itself. The message body may be a query string, or a block of data, such as occurrences that a user has modified in the browser and now wants to store. Uniface transparently converts HTTP requests into data structures that can be interpreted by the server page components, and converts the generated response back into an HTTP response, ProcScript commands and functions in server page components are used to read and process the requests and generate the responses. For more information, see ProcScript for Dynamic Server Pages and ProcScript for Static Server Pages . (Static server pages can also be used to enable file downloads, so the message could conceivably be a file.) The response of a dynamic server page is a complete HTML page the first time the component is requested, but thereafter requests it may contain only the part of the page that has changed. When the client requests a DSP for the first time, the generated response includes a JavaScript runtime engine that manages and synchronizes subsequent communication between the client and server. It can submit requests to activate server-side Proc modules, and execute responses from the server to execute for client-side JavaScript. For more information, see DSP Component Instances . HTTP Communication Between Client and Server The following illustration shows the communication flow in the request-response cycle of a Uniface web or mobile application. Figure 1. Uniface HTTP Processing A user agent initiates an HTTP request for a Uniface component by means of a URL. For more information, see Uniface URL Format . The web server activates the servlet engine, which instantiates the Web Request Dispatcher (WRD). For more information, see Web Request Dispatcher (WRD) . The WRD converts the HTTP request to the parameters required to activate the Uniface component, and dispatches it to the Uniface Router using the credentials specified in the WRD's configuration file ( web.xml ). The Uniface Router checks the credentials and forwards the request to the Uniface Server. The Uniface Server activates the USYSHTTP component, which parses the HTTP headers and activates the component specified in the HTTP request. For more information, see USYSHTTP . If the component cannot be activated, it generates a response containing the error and technical details. (The contents and appearance of this error page can be configured using logicals. For more information, see Web Application Error Pages ). The component loads the request (using webget in USPs and webload in DSPs), and performs the requested actions. The component may require data from the database, which it accesses via a DBMS connector. The component then generates a response (using webgen in USPs and websave in DSPs). (The $webinfo ProcScript function can be used to access the header, data, and other information required to interpret requests and generate responses. For more information, see $webinfo ). The Uniface Server then returns the response via Uniface Router to the WRD Servlet. The WRD converts the response to an HTTP response and sends it to the HTTP client. If the client is a web browser, it renders and displays the result.
